reliability_note.finding: All 7 required endpoints returned 200 in this snapshot, but the backend is exhibiting an intermittent crash-restart cycle (~5 min period) under real market-hours load, separate from the OOM bug already fixed in this session (PR #53). Multiple capture attempts during this proof session returned 502/000 before this successful snapshot.
reliability_note.root_cause_hypothesis: core/data/instruments_cache.py's InstrumentsCache is a correct singleton (loads once, no per-request leak) but permanently holds a 121,152-row pandas DataFrame in memory once triggered by a real chain/instrument lookup — only happens during actual market hours, not during off-hours testing. Combined with FastAPI baseline + per-request pandas processing, this appears to push a 512Mi container close to its ceiling; any additional transient load then triggers an OOM kill.
reliability_note.not_fixed_this_session: This is a capacity-shaped issue, not a discrete bug like the zombie-thread fix (PR #53). Recommended follow-up: upgrade the Render plan for more RAM, or a separate effort to reduce the instrument DataFrame's memory footprint (column pruning, more efficient dtypes).
reliability_note.evidence: Render memory metrics showed repeated climbs to 250-380MB (down from ~500MB pre-fix) followed by resets, on a roughly 5-minute cycle during live market hours (2026-07-02 ~12:00-16:25 IST).
